substitutes.name: Dry Sherry
substitutes.notes: Closest in flavor profile. Use fino or manzanilla sherry.

substitutes.name: Dry Vermouth
substitutes.notes: Herbal notes differ but works well in stir-fries.

substitutes.name: Mirin + Rice Vinegar
substitutes.notes: Sweeter profile. Reduce other sugars in recipe.
substitutes.compensation: Reduce sugar by 1 tsp per tbsp of substitute

substitutes.name: Gochujang
substitutes.notes: Korean chili paste. Sweeter and less fermented.
substitutes.compensation: Reduce salt by 25%, add a splash of soy sauce

substitutes.name: Sambal Oelek + Miso
substitutes.notes: Approximates the chili-fermented bean flavor.

substitutes.name: Sichuan Peppercorn Oil
substitutes.ratio: 1 tsp oil per 1/2 tsp whole peppercorn
substitutes.notes: Add at end of cooking for best numbing effect.

substitutes.name: Black Pepper + Lemon Zest
substitutes.notes: Won't replicate the numbing sensation but adds complexity.

substitutes.name: Mushroom Oyster Sauce
substitutes.notes: Vegetarian version. Nearly identical in use.

substitutes.name: Soy Sauce + Sugar
substitutes.ratio: 1 tbsp soy + 1/2 tsp sugar per 1 tbsp oyster sauce
substitutes.notes: Misses the depth but provides similar saltiness and sweetness.

substitutes.name: Regular Soy Sauce + Molasses
substitutes.notes: Replicates the color and slight sweetness.

substitutes.name: Double Black Soy Sauce
substitutes.notes: Even darker and thicker. Use slightly less.

substitutes.name: Japanese Soy Sauce (Koikuchi)
substitutes.notes: Slightly different fermentation but very close.

substitutes.name: Tamari
substitutes.notes: Gluten-free option. Slightly richer and less salty.

substitutes.name: Balsamic Vinegar
substitutes.notes: Sweeter and fruitier, but closest Western substitute.

substitutes.name: Rice Vinegar + Worcestershire
substitutes.notes: Adds complexity that plain rice vinegar lacks.

substitutes.name: Black Bean Garlic Sauce
substitutes.notes: Pre-made sauce containing douchi. Use slightly more.

substitutes.name: Miso Paste
substitutes.notes: Different bean base but provides fermented umami.

substitutes.name: Chile de Arbol
substitutes.notes: Similar heat level and thin-walled texture.

substitutes.name: Red Pepper Flakes
substitutes.notes: Less visual impact but provides the heat.

substitutes.name: DIY Blend
substitutes.ratio: Equal parts star anise, cloves, cinnamon, Sichuan peppercorn, fennel seeds
substitutes.notes: Grind fresh for best results.

substitutes.name: Allspice + Cinnamon
substitutes.ratio: 3/4 tsp allspice + 1/4 tsp cinnamon per 1 tsp five-spice
substitutes.notes: Missing key notes but works in a pinch.

substitutes.name: Potato Starch
substitutes.notes: Best substitute for velveting and thickening.

substitutes.name: Tapioca Starch
substitutes.notes: Creates a slightly chewier texture. Good for coating.

substitutes.name: Regular Doubanjiang
substitutes.notes: Less complex but functionally equivalent.

substitutes.name: Gochujang + Miso
substitutes.notes: Approximates the fermented chili bean flavor.
substitutes.compensation: Add a splash of soy sauce
